---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# PmetricsReports

  <!-- badges: start -->
  [![R-CMD-check](https://github.com/LAPKB/PmetricsReports/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/LAPKB/PmetricsReports/actions/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml)

  <!-- badges: end -->

`PmetricsReports` is a Shiny companion app for reviewing `PM_result` objects and creating PDF reports after fitting data to models.

## Installation

Install `PmetricsReports` directly from [r-universe](https://lapkb.r-universe.dev/PmetricsReports) or from the repository source.

### If automatic installation fails...

You can also manually install `PmetricsReports` from [r-universe](https://lapkb.r-universe.dev/PmetricsReports). To install, you must specify `r-universe` as the repository using

```r
install.packages("PmetricsReports", repos = "https://lapkb.r-universe.dev")
```

For updating the package and for easier future manual installations, you can run the following command
to add the `r-universe` repository to your `R.profile`

```r
write('options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org", LAPKB = "https://lapkb.r-universe.dev"))', "~/.Rprofile", append = TRUE)
```

With this setup, you can install or update `PmetricsReports` using the
standard command `install.packages("PmetricsReports")` without specifying
the repository each time.


<!-- You'll still need to render `README.Rmd` regularly, to keep `README.md` up-to-date. `devtools::build_readme()` is handy for this. -->
